paragraphs.body: In the article “What is known (and not known) about Area 51”, The Editors’ of the Encyclopaedia Britannica lists numerous facts about the mysteries behind Area 51. They begin by listing many general facts about Area 51 such as where its located, different nicknames for its facilities, and what its function is believed to be. But what makes this facility different from any other governmental facility. First, they mention that locals have witnessed UFO sightings at the Area 51 facility from as early as 1956. The research states that it was initially used for creating and testing military weapons. Next, they dive into the conspiracy theories about extraterrestrial life. Area 51 has been the breeding ground for many conspiracy theories over the last couple of decades. I guess my main point comes from many hypothetical questions that I used to ask my brothers when we had these sorts of conversations. These funny, silly, meaningless conversations led to some of our deepest conversations about our goals, dreams and our family's future. This sparked many of our greatest ideas which we are now putting into place in the real world. Discussing the topic of Area 51 we asked ourselves ‘How high can we go’, not regarding the UFOs at Area 51 but regarding our ceilings in this life.

paragraphs.body: Essay 1 Beginning As an amused conspiracy theorist, I have had an avid curiosity about Area 51. To many people Area 51 is nothing but an army base for the US government. But to a small majority of people Area 51 was the hub for one of the most popular conspiracy theories globally. In the article “Area 51: What is it and what goes on there?” Robert Lea lists numerous facts about the mysteries behind the Area 51. Lea begins by listing many general facts about Area 51 such as where its located, different nicknames for the facility, its influence in pop culture and what the facility is used for. But what makes this facility different from any other governmental facility in the US? What if it is more than a government facility?

annotations.text: the article “Area 51: What is it and what goes on there?” Robert Lea lists numerous facts about the mysteries behind the Area 51. Lea begins by listing many general facts about Area 51 such as where its located, different nicknames for the facility, its influence in pop culture and what the facility is used for.
annotations.comment: Aha. Here's where your summary actually begins. Perhaps you don't need that first paragraph at all?

paragraphs.body: Robert begins by writing about UFOs (Unidentified Flying Objects), with the earliest sighting dating back to 1956. This does not necessarily mean that a UFO is an alien spacecraft but may just be a new innovative style that may benefit the design of the model. There is an American investigator and writer called Benjamin Radford. He is a keen conspiracy theorist who has written several books about all sorts of difference conspiracy theories. His most notable being “Scientific Paranormal Investigation: How to solve Unexplained Mysteries”, “Tracking the Chupacabra: The Vampire Beast in Fact, Fiction and Folklore” and “Mysterious New Mexico: Miracles, Magic and Monsters in the Land of Enchantment”. In a recent article discussing Area 51, Benjamin Radford suggested that strange lights do not claim that there is a UFO. Benjamin Radford argues, “It’s true that strange lights and aircraft can sometimes be spotted in the area, so its obvious leap to UFOs, but of course, new aircraft might look identical.” In other words, Radford believes that we are on the outside looking in so we cannot make any assumptions about what may or may not be. Many people will be quick to think the worst and assume that its a UFO and aliens may possibly be on board. But he wants people to remember the likelihood of such an occurrence happening.

annotations.text: In a recent article discussing Area 51, Benjamin Radford suggested that strange lights do not claim that there is a UFO. Benjamin Radford argues, “It’s true that strange lights and aircraft can sometimes be spotted in the area, so its obvious leap to UFOs, but of course, new aircraft might look identical.” In other words, Radford believes that we are on the outside looking in so we cannot make any assumptions about what may or may not be.

paragraphs.body: Although this does scratch off the possibility that an occurrence like that is an alien aircraft. For example, there was incident in Rosewell, New Mexico back in 1947. A local rancher, called William Brazel, woke up to find remnants of what he just thought was plane crash. Brazel was not familiar with UFOs or flying saucers. However, sightings were not uncommon in the summer of 1947. Just a month prior to this crash, a pilot named Kenneth Arnold reported that he had seen 9 unidentified flying objects flying at an estimated 1200 mph. This was nearly double the speed that the fastest plane at the time was travelling. To have 9 of those UFOs flying at the same time next to each other is extremely rare. This began to raise eyebrows about the situation and got more people involved in the search for these flying saucers. With all the popularity around the sighting, the government were struggling to keep William Brazel’s crash quiet. In an article called Roswell UFO Crash: What is the truth behind the ‘flying saucer’ incident? David Crookes reported the government's attempt to cover up any evidence. Crookes claimed, “an equally intrigued Wilcox contacted Colonel William Blanchard, the commanding officer of the Roswell Army Air Field (RAAF), who sent agents to the site to gather the remaining material”. In making this comment, Crookes is illustrating the secrecy of the government. Sheriff Wilcox is even working with the army officials to keep it undercover. Government secrecy is another reason why many people jump to conclusions about what's going on. Expanding on the point I made in my last paragraph is that we are on the outside looking in. But the government is doing a great job of keeping the unknown as the unknown.

annotations.text: In making this comment, Crookes is illustrating the secrecy of the government.

paragraphs.body: The Area 51 facility is small, but the surrounding area is enormous. That can partially be due to the fact they need space to fly planes or do they need space to test weapons. If it is only a governmental facility, why are there signs claiming that deadly force will be used for any people trespassing. Pretty strange for any regular government facility, am I right?

I guess maybe this conspiracy is all about perception. To the non-conspirators it is just the Nevada Test and Training Range but to everyone else it is THE AREA 51. Perception is a way of regarding, understanding or interpreting something. I guess I used you to be one of those people that thought it was nonsense. However, both of my brothers were more eager in learning about the unearthed secrets about all sorts of conspiracy theories. When I began to listen to the conversations, I slowly started to expand my mind and ask myself the hypothetical question of ‘What If?’. My whole perception had changed because instead of looking for something for what it may be, I would dive deeper into the topic to learn more. My brothers and I began to expand our horizon and research about different topics with all of us gaining a wealth of knowledge. We soon transformed our conversations from these silly, meaningless chats to talking about our goals, dreams, and our family's future. This sparked many of our greatest ideas which we are now putting into the real world. We went from discussing how high can UFOs but we then asked ourselves, how high can our dreams go? References: https://www.livescience.com/roswell-ufo-crash-what-really-happened.html David Crookes, All About Space Magazine 5-6-2021 https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flight_airspeed_record Note- mention pop culture influence
